ssid.name: Wireless: SSID
ssid.description: The name of the WiFi network you want to host.

wpa_passphrase.name: Wireless: WPA2 Password
wpa_passphrase.description: The password for the WiFi network. Must be at least 8 characters long.

channel.name: Wireless: 2.4GHz Channel
channel.description: The channel to broadcast on. If unsure; either 1, 6 or 11 is recommended.

address.name: LAN: AP Address
address.description: IP address of the Home Assistant host on the AP subnet. In router mode this device is the gateway; in access point mode it is the relay/router towards upstream.

netmask.name: LAN: Netmask
netmask.description: The netmask of the hosted subnet. Leave default if uncertain.

broadcast.name: LAN: Broadcast Address
broadcast.description: The broadcast address of the network. This must always be the last address in the subnet. Leave default if unsure.

interface.name: Wireless: Interface
interface.description: The wireless network interface to use to host the network. On a Raspberry Pi, the builtin one will be wlan0 (default).

network_mode.name: Device Behavior
network_mode.description: Use 'offline' for a local AP subnet with DHCP/NAT, or 'online' for a transparent AP that joins the upstream LAN. Legacy values 'router', 'access_point' and 'bridge' are accepted for compatibility.

bridge_interface.name: Advanced: Uplink Device Name
bridge_interface.description: Advanced internal device name used when the AP is placed online. Leave untouched unless you know why it matters.

upstream_interface.name: Advanced: Uplink Interface Override
upstream_interface.description: Optional manual override for the uplink interface. Leave empty to detect it automatically from the host routing table.

hide_ssid.name: Hide Network SSID
hide_ssid.description: Whether to hide the network SSID. Use with caution.

dhcp.name: DHCP: Enable Local Server
dhcp.description: Enable the local DNSMASQ DHCP server on the AP subnet. In online mode this is ignored because the upstream router provides DHCP.

dhcp_start_addr.name: DHCP: Range Start
dhcp_start_addr.description: The start of the IP address range to automatically assign to devices.

dhcp_end_addr.name: DHCP: Range End
dhcp_end_addr.description: The end of the IP address range to automatically assign to devices.

dhcp_relay_server.name: DHCP: Upstream Router / Server
dhcp_relay_server.description: Optional upstream DHCP server IP for offline relay setups. In online mode the upstream LAN handles DHCP directly.

allow_mac_addresses.name: MAC Address Allowlist
allow_mac_addresses.description: A list of the MAC addresses that are allowed to connect to the network. Any other devices will be blocked. This overrides the 'MAC Address Blocklist' option.

deny_mac_addresses.name: MAC Address Blocklist
deny_mac_addresses.description: A list of the MAC addresses that are NOT allowed to connect to the network. Any other devices will be allowed.

hostapd_config_override.name: HOSTAPD Custom Configuration
hostapd_config_override.description: A list of options to append to the `hostapd.conf` file. Don't touch if you don't know what you're doing.

client_internet_access.name: Routing: Enable Client Internet Access
client_internet_access.description: Allow traffic from AP clients to leave through the detected uplink. Offline mode adds NAT; online mode is handled by the upstream LAN.

client_dns_override.name: DHCP: Client DNS Override
client_dns_override.description: A list of DNS server IPs to use. If empty, will use Homeassistant's default DNS servers.

dnsmasq_config_override.name: Advanced: DNSMASQ Custom Configuration
dnsmasq_config_override.description: A list of options to append to the `dnsmasq.conf` file. Don't touch if you don't know what you're doing.
